findings.evidence: New proofs at BoundedPerturbation.lean:71,84,96,107,117 are one-line applications of existing declarations. Existing grep hits: UniformEllipticity.lean:612 `lemma add_bounded`; UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:165 `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_self`, :194 `isCoercive_energyIntegrand`, :207 `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_on`, :219 `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift`, :232 `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift_on`.
findings.fix: Remove these wrappers, or justify a broader API pattern; use `(h.add_bounded hMu_nonneg hMu_lt hp).min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_self`, `.isCoercive_energyIntegrand`, `.isCoercive_energyIntegrand_on`, `.isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift`, and `.isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift_on` at call sites.
findings.issue: All five new declarations duplicate the existing composition `h.add_bounded ...` followed by existing energy coercivity lemmas, creating a parallel perturbation-specific API.

summary: The added file introduces only thin composed wrappers around the existing bounded-perturbation ellipticity theorem and the existing energy coercivity consumers. This is parallel API rather than new material; callers can use `h.add_bounded ...` with the existing `_energyIntegrand...` lemmas directly.
